full_text: It was actually the day before I started high school. I got my period, so that was just completely not a good time at all. And it was actually the first day I was in PE. Normally I was doing track, I was doing dance and I'd done that since two years old, never had any issues. And then randomly on my first day we would do a PE and I fainted. I remember being in such debilitating pain. I got sent to school nurse at the time. I just remember looking down and I was bleeding everywhere and I just remember thinking like why am I soaking through pads and having to change my pads, you know, every ten minutes but at the time because it was the first day of my period I'd never ever experienced this before. I just thought oh you know maybe it's normal and you know I remember telling the teachers like oh like I need to get changed or I really don't feel well and they were like oh no you know you'll just get used to it and I was like but I'm in like severe pain and I felt like I couldn't really ask anyone else because they either hadn't gotten their period yet or if they had you know it wasn't any different to normal day life. But I remember thinking like paracetamol wasn't helping, I was passing out, I was dizzy, I was having to change my pad every ten minutes and you know going to school then you know like from primary school I was in every day, my attendance was always fine and then at high school it just dipped because at every time I'd get my period which was never regular either, it would be all over the place and I either would be sent home from school and be in the school nurse's office, I couldn't go to dance lessons and that was really unheard of from me. So that was when me myself and my family were like this isn't right so then that was when I went to the GP and kind of said I'm really unwell around my periods like nobody else seems to either be hospitalised or bedbound by their period like what's going on and I think the first few visits it was like oh you know you'll just get used to it you know it's because it's your first period you know and I just thought oh I'd go for medical help and everyone tells me you know it's fine that you'll get used to it and I definitely believe that for quite some time but it just got worse and worse and yeah.
